summary: two subject builders (subjects.py vs jetstream/stream_config.py), nothing forces byte-identical
P2 holds a CommandHandler ref and calls handle() in-process instead of publishing
P3 in-memory timers lost on restart; quiesced candidate-closed aggregate never re-arms
no guard against ValidationError on event deserialization; malformed stored event crashes load/subscribe
dispatcher just logs failing projectors; no DLQ, no max-retry, no poison detection
ConcurrencyError swallowed with bare pass; in-memory has no replay loop so child never finalizes
subscriber asyncio.Queues unbounded; slow projector backs up memory with no backpressure
test_dedup_was_per_call_in_sprint_2 pins 'harmless duplicate' as required; blocks correct fix
~80% functional; adversarial tests cover only the easy cases
sleeps 0.35s for a 0.1s horizon; publish round-trip can exceed slack under CI load
_find_event_by_command_id self-documents O(n) Sprint-2 scan; documented deferral
non-durable push sub, no from_event_id; cold-start replay unsupported, deliver-policy drift
code is created->active->finalized; brief mandates open<->candidate-closed->finalized + retraction (run-2 headline)
EventEnvelope/CommandMessage live in contrib/events/envelopes.py, not core/messages.py per brief
fixtures reject InternalFinalize although brief says it MUST NOT define a rejection path
forged-issuer raises CorruptAggregateAlert, a 4th exception type outside brief's allowable set
real CAS correctness bug: subject includes event_type so per-subject seq != per-aggregate version
